page: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the Dalv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU, Geekbench single-core, and text processing scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls into the average range. However, memory performance is a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score is in the bottom 10%, and memory bandwidth read-only tests for both small and large blocks rank in the bottom 25%. LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but drop to poor or weak levels for medium 7B models.

Given its low memory-to-CPU ratio of 1:2, the D4als_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints or local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation and strong single-core performance make it suitable for tasks such as static web serving, text processing, and lightweight Redis key-value operations. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, or large-scale language model inference due to its constrained memory bandwidth and 8.0 GB RAM capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is a dedicated x86_64 virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of system memory, and no local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks. However, its memory subsystem is a bottleneck, with memory bandwidth and Passmark memory scores falling into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. This low-memory configuration makes the D4als_v6 a cost-efficient choice for compute-focused workloads with small memory footprints, such as static web serving, text processing, and basic Redis operations, while rendering it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or complex software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is a dedicated 4-vCPU virtual machine powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for compute-heavy workloads with low memory requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 features 4 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU, and 8.0 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for low-memory workloads.
tagline: A dedicated four-vCPU AMD EPYC compute instance designed for single-core efficiency and low-memory workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance ranking in the top 25%.
No local storage included, but compatible with Premium Storage options.
Best suited for static web serving, text processing, and lightweight Redis operations.
